DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO PLTF S CLARKE'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE FILED BY DEFENDANT REID, PAT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF S. CLARK SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT PAT REID'S CROSS-COMPLAINT BY PLAINTIFF S. CLARK, MINOR BY JACQUELINE REID UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 425.16 ("ANTI-SLAPP" MOTION). COUNSEL STATED HE GAVE NOTICE, PURSUANT TO SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 8.3. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. THE CONDUCT DESCRIBED IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS NOT PROTECTED SPEECH OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CROSS-DEFENDANT DOES NOT SPECIFY THE SPECIFIC SUBSECTION OF CCP SECTION 425.16(E) UNDER WHICH HER ACTIONS QUALIFY. NO EVIDENCE SUPPORTS ALLEGATION THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS PART OF A POLICE INVESTIGATION OR DONE IN SERIOUS CONTEMPLATION OF LITIGATION. THE CONDUCT DID NOT CONCERN A MATTER OF PUBLIC INTEREST, BUT WAS A PRIVATE MATTER. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAD NOT BEEN CHARGED WITH A CRIME OR EVEN DETAINED BY THE POLICE. THE ALLEGED ASSAULT OCCURRED IN A PRIVATE RESIDENCE BETWEEN TWO INDIVIDUALS, WHILE THE FLIERS WERE POSTED NOT ONLY IN CROSS-COMPLAINANTS IMMEDIATE NEIGHBORHOOD IN SOUTH BEACH, BUT ALSO IN THE MISSION. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: ANGIE DINER, CSR 	581
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Defendant Pat Reid'S Cross-Complaint By Plaintiff S. Clark, Minor By Jacqueline Reid Under Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16 ("Anti-Slapp" Motion)
Off Calendar Apr-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF S. CLARK Special Motion to Strike Defendant Pat Reid's Cross-Complaint By Plaintiff S. Clark, Minor By Jacqueline Reid Under Code of Civil Procedure 425.16 ("Anti-Slapp" Motion). Appearances: Don C. Burns, Esq. Attorney at Law, (949) 661-1038 Appearing thru CourtCall for Plaintiff and Moving party. There are no other appearances. Counsel stated he gave notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. The conduct described in the cross-complaint was not protected speech or petitioning activity. Cross-defendant does not specify the specific subsection of CCP section 425.16(e) under which her actions qualify. No evidence supports allegation that cross-defendant's conduct was part of a police investigation or done in serious contemplation of litigation. The conduct did not concern a matter of public interest, but was a private matter. Cross-complainant had not been charged with a crime or even detained by the police. The alleged assault occurred in a private residence between two individuals, while the fliers were posted not only in cross-complainants immediate neighborhood in South Beach, but also in the Mission. Request for attorney fees denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Angie Diner CSR# 9581
